title: Higginsia regalis
intro: Higginsia regalis, also known as Royal Sponges or Royal Coral, is a species of marine demosponge that belongs to the family Higginsiidae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in wound healing, reducing the risk of infections, and protecting against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Higginsia regalis include various compounds such as sterols, triterpenes, and other polyphenolic metabolites that contribute to its therapeutic properties. Higginsia regalis is typically processed into herbal preparations such as infusions, extracts, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal benefits and support overall well-being.
taxonomy: Higginsia regalis is classified as a species of sponge in the kingdom Animalia, phylum Porifera, and class Calcarea. It belongs to the order Clathrinida and the family Clathrinidae. This classification suggests that Higginsia regalis is a marine organism with a simple body structure and a sponge-like appearance. The genus Higginsia is thought to be closely related to other calcareous sponges. Higginsia regalis is also known to be a relatively rare species found in certain parts of the ocean.
